common.indicativeNote: indicative countervalue at the frozen rate
common.loginRequired: You need to sign in to continue.

home.title: Ship parcels from person to person
home.intro: Mercurio connects people shipping low-value, non-urgent parcels with occasional carriers and neighbourhood hubs. Payments run directly between users on Lightning: the platform never holds funds.
home.sendBody: Create a shipment, print the QR and drop it at the origin hub.
home.carrierBody: Declare your trip and earn by carrying parcels along your way.
home.hubsBody: Discover the active deposit points in the network.
home.recentEmpty: You haven't created any shipments yet.

auth.loginIntro: We email you a sign-in link. No passwords.
auth.sentBody: If the address is correct you will receive a sign-in link valid for 15 minutes.
auth.consentBody: This address is signing in for the first time: creating the account requires accepting the Terms of Service and the privacy policy (GDPR).
auth.consentReadNote: They open in a new tab: this page stays valid.
auth.consentCheckbox: I accept the Terms of Service and the privacy policy
auth.consentSpecificCheckbox: I specifically approve the clauses listed in ToS §15 (artt. 1341-1342 of the Italian Civil Code): the Operator not being a party, liability following certified custody, deterministic outcomes with no arbiter and no insurance, pickup as final acceptance, end-of-storage release, liability limitations, amendments and venue.

errors.token_expired: Link expired: request a new one.
errors.token_already_used: Link already used: request a new one.
errors.rate_limited: Too many attempts: try again later.

wallet.title: Your Lightning wallet
wallet.intro: Mercurio never holds funds: you connect YOUR wallet, which pays and collects directly from your counterparties. Required before sending or carrying.
wallet.replaceNote: Connecting a new wallet replaces the previous one.
wallet.nwcHint: Paste your wallet's NWC string (nostr+walletconnect://…). Sending, carrying or running a hub needs a wallet with hold-invoice support (e.g. Alby Hub): we check this when you connect.
wallet.lndHint: Your LND node reachable over REST (in development: the regtest nodes from infra/docker).
wallet.fakeHint: Available only when the API runs with FAKE_WALLETS=true (e.g. "alice").

hubs.title: Network hubs
hubs.intro: Shops and bars that hold parcels in transit in exchange for a percentage of each leg's gross.
hubs.mineBody: Do you run a business with long opening hours?
hubs.maxStorage: Max storage {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}
hubs.walletOff: Wallet not connected: cannot receive parcels
hubs.empty: No active hubs at the moment.

send.title: Send a parcel
send.needWallet: Connect your Lightning wallet before creating a shipment: payment holds are issued from it.
send.recipientHint: They will receive updates and the pickup code.
send.contentHint: Declaring it helps hubs accept the parcel.
send.undeclaredLabel: I am not declaring the content
send.undeclaredHint: Some hubs do not accept parcels with undeclared content.
send.storageHint: Once storage expires the parcel becomes releasable to the hub under the Terms of Service (§10): you get an email warning 72 and 24 hours ahead, and a 7-day window to recover it remains. Maximum {max} days.
send.offerHint: A spending commitment paid leg by leg: the more you offer, the higher you rank on carriers' boards.
send.suggestionBody: Similar shipments were delivered at about {eur} ({sats} sats) over {km}.
send.suggestionPriority: Offer more to gain priority on the boards.
send.bondHint: Required from whoever holds the parcel (carrier or hub); released on certified delivery. Maximum €1,000.

shipment.title: Shipment
shipment.notFound: Shipment not found (or you are not a participant).
shipment.createdAccepted: The origin hub has already accepted and locked its bond.
shipment.createdNotAccepted: Waiting for the origin hub to accept.
shipment.qrInstructions: Print the QR, stick it on the parcel, then bring it to the origin hub.
shipment.poolHint: 90% of the commitment spread over the remaining km; 10% is the delivery bonus.
shipment.legsEmpty: No legs yet: the parcel is waiting for a carrier on the board.
shipment.boostHint: Raises the commitment for future legs. No immediate movement.
shipment.rerouteHint: Changes the destination hub and/or the recipient; the pickup code is regenerated.
shipment.rerouteNeedChange: Provide a new hub or a new recipient.
shipment.cancelBody: Cancelling pays the origin hub its compensation and unlocks the parcel's return. Continue?

DRAFT.label: Draft
DRAFT.description: Created, waiting for the origin hub to accept.

AWAITING_DROPOFF.label: Drop off at the hub
AWAITING_DROPOFF.description: The origin hub accepted: bring the parcel with the QR attached.

AT_HUB.label: At hub
AT_HUB.description: The parcel sits at a hub, waiting for a carrier.

LEG_BOOKED.label: Leg booked
LEG_BOOKED.description: A carrier accepted: holds being funded, pickup imminent.

IN_TRANSIT.label: In transit
IN_TRANSIT.description: The parcel is with the carrier, heading to the next hub.

AWAITING_PICKUP.label: Ready for pickup
AWAITING_PICKUP.description: Arrived at destination: the recipient can collect it with the OTP code.

CLAIMED.label: Claimed
CLAIMED.description: The recipient claimed the parcel and will come to collect it at the hub.

DELIVERED.label: Delivered
DELIVERED.description: The recipient collected the parcel. Shipment closed.

CANCELLED.label: Cancelled
CANCELLED.description: Cancelled by the sender before the first pickup.

FORFEITED.label: Forfeited
FORFEITED.description: Storage expired: the parcel is released to the hub under the Terms of Service (§10). The sender has 7 days to recover it by paying the extra storage directly to the hub.

LOST.label: Lost
LOST.description: Transit timeout: the carrier's bond was collected by the sender.

custody.leg_returned: Parcel returned to the departure hub

carrier.title: Carry and earn
carrier.intro: Declare your real trip: the board highlights the shipments worth your while along the way.
carrier.needWallet: Connect your Lightning wallet before accepting legs: the bond is issued from it.
carrier.rateLabel: Minimum rate (sats per km of detour)
carrier.rateSuggestion: Carriers in your area accepted on average {eur}/km ({sats} sats/km).

validation.deviation: Enter a detour between 1 and 500 km.

board.title: Board
board.forYouEmpty: No shipment matches your criteria right now.
board.othersEmpty: No other shipments in the area.
board.empty: No shipments available along your trip.
board.acceptBody: You pick up from {from} and deliver to {to}. Amounts are frozen at acceptance: no surprises after.
board.acceptedBody: The holds must be funded by {time}: keep your wallet reachable.

route.title: Your route
route.subtitle: Stops in the shortest visit order; pickup always precedes drop.
route.googleNote: No data is sent to Google until you open the link.
route.stopsEmpty: No stops: accept a leg from the board or open a preview.
route.unroutedBody: The itinerary routes at most {max} stops: plan these separately.

hub.title: Your hub
hub.registerIntro: A hub is a business with long opening hours that holds parcels in transit in exchange for a percentage of each leg's gross. Declare your constraints once: compatible requests will come by themselves.
hub.contactEmailHint: Distinct from your account email: deposit-request notices go here. Leave empty to use the account email.
hub.coordsHint: The coordinates place the hub in leg pricing and on the carriers' map.
hub.hoursHint: Leave closing days empty. At least one day is required.
hub.storageHint: Past the storage window the parcel becomes releasable in your favor under the Terms of Service (§10: recovery window and valuation with any excess owed to the sender). Maximum {max} days.
hub.undeclaredLabel: I accept parcels with undeclared content
hub.feeHint: Withheld from the gross of every leg departing from or arriving at your hub. Maximum {max}%.
hub.autoAcceptHint: You automatically accept deposits that fit your declared constraints: required to receive parcels as a leg's arrival hub.

validation.contactEmail: The contact email is not valid.
validation.hours: Provide the hours of at least one day.
validation.dims: Dimensions and weight must be positive integers.
validation.fee: The percentage must be between 0 and {max}.
validation.storage: Storage must be between 1 and {max} days.

hub.needWallet: Connect your Lightning wallet: the custody bond of every deposit is issued from it.
hub.acceptIntro: Shipments asking to depart from your hub: accepting locks your bond until a carrier picks the parcel up.
hub.storageLine: max storage {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}
hub.staysIntro: Parcels reserved or in custody at your hub, with their storage deadlines.
hub.venueIntro: Show your venue to senders and carriers. Up to {max} photos; metadata (location) is stripped on the device before upload.

hubOps.title: Parcel operations
hubOps.qrHint: Scan the QR on the parcel and paste the result here (the full URL works too).
hubOps.nothingToDo: No operation available for your hub in this state.
hubOps.acceptIntro: Accepting locks your custody bond: it is released when a carrier picks the parcel up.
hubOps.acceptDone: Deposit accepted: bond locked, the sender can bring you the parcel.
hubOps.checkinIntro: The sender hands you the parcel: verify the QR and photograph it. The storage timer starts here.
hubOps.checkinDone: Check-in recorded: the parcel is in storage and appears on the boards.
hubOps.checkoutIntro: Double confirmation: you certify with photos what you hand over, the carrier confirms from their app within 15 minutes. Custody changes only with both confirmations.
hubOps.checkoutWaitingCarrier: Confirmation recorded: waiting for the carrier's confirmation.
hubOps.checkoutComplete: Check-out complete: the parcel is on its way.
hubOps.arrivalIntro: Inspect the parcel before certifying: from this moment the responsibility is yours. If you cannot certify its integrity, reject the handoff below.
hubOps.integrityLabel: I confirm the parcel is intact
hubOps.integrityHint: Certifying releases the carrier's payout and engages your bond.
hubOps.arrivalDone: Arrival certified: the carrier collects and the parcel is in storage with you.
hubOps.returnIntro: The carrier brings back the parcel they picked up from you: you are bound to take it back (Terms of Service §8). The leg payment and the carrier bond are cancelled.
hubOps.returnDone: Return recorded: the parcel is in storage with you again.
hubOps.pickupIntro: Have the parcel inspected BEFORE typing the code: the OTP is the definitive acceptance, with no contestation window.
hubOps.otpHint: The recipient received it by email when the parcel arrived.
hubOps.claimedIntro: The recipient claimed the parcel: verify their personal code. Accepting is definitive, exactly like the OTP.
hubOps.claimTokenHint: The recipient shows it from their device (text or QR).
hubOps.rejectIntro: If the parcel is not in the expected condition, document it with photos and a reason: custody does NOT pass, the state does not change and the sender is notified (no arbiter, documentation only).
hubOps.rejectDone: Rejection filed: the sender has been notified.

reviews.title: Review the hubs
reviews.intro: The shipment is closed: you have 30 days to review the hubs that held it. Only hubs are reviewable — the one counterparty you choose and meet again.
reviews.subjectLabel: Which hub do you want to review?
reviews.commentHint: Reviews are public and can be neither edited nor deleted.

profile.title: Public profile
profile.ratingsHint: Average computed on read over every review received as a hub (ADR-027).

account.title: Your account
account.exportBody: You download one JSON document with everything the platform keeps about you: account, hub, trips, shipments, reviews written and received, consent history.
account.deleteBody: Deletion anonymizes your account and revokes every session: your email and personal data are unlinked forever. The ledger and the custody chain remain (they hold no personal data); a hub you own is deactivated. This cannot be undone.
account.deleteConfirmLabel: I understand: this cannot be undone.

legal.operator: Operator of this instance (and data controller): to be filled in at instance deploy time.
legal.note: Courtesy translation: the Italian version prevails.

sections.title: 1. What Mercurio is, who the Operator is
sections.body:
Mercurio is open source software coordinating a peer-to-peer logistics network: senders entrust parcels to drop-off points (hubs) and occasional travellers (carriers), who move them towards the destination leg by leg. Payments happen on the Lightning Network directly between users.

The Operator is the party running this instance of Mercurio, identified at the bottom of this page. The Operator provides the coordination software: it is not a party to the contracts between users (§3), it never holds funds (§5), it does not decide disputes (§8). The coordination service is free: the platform fee is 0%.

sections.title: 2. Roles and definitions
sections.body:
– Sender: creates the shipment, seals the parcel and hands it to the origin hub.
– Hub: a business with long opening hours that stores parcels in transit in exchange for a percentage, configured in its profile, of the gross of every adjacent leg.
– Carrier: picks shipments from the board and moves them between hubs, even for a partial leg only.
– Recipient: receives email updates and collects the parcel at destination (free, no account needed); with an account and a wallet they can also collect it early from a hub along the route (§9).

One account can hold every role. The custody chain is the immutable record of every shipment event: the documentary proof of who certified what. The custody bond is the amount, set by the sender, that whoever takes custody of the parcel locks as a guarantee (§5).

sections.title: 3. The contract is between peers; the platform is not a party
sections.body:
Every shipment gives rise to a logistics contract concluded directly between the sender, the hubs and the carriers who take part in it: the hub's acceptance, the carrier's leg acceptance and every certification at a handoff are the parties' declarations of intent, recorded in the custody chain. Custody at a hub is a paid deposit (artt. 1766 ff. of the Italian Civil Code) ancillary to that contract.

The Operator undertakes no transport, storage or delivery obligation; it does not vet parcels, users or offers in advance; it does not warrant that a shipment will find carriers or reach its destination.

sections.title: 4. Access requirements
sections.body:
The service is restricted to adults, with a verified email address. Whoever moves money (sender, carrier, hub, recipient collecting early) must connect their own Lightning wallet with hold-invoice support: the platform probes its capabilities when it is connected and has no access to the funds.

On Lightning the payer and the payee must be different users: the sender, the leg's carrier and the owners of the hubs involved in one shipment must be distinct accounts. Acceptance of these Terms and of the privacy policy happens at account creation and is recorded with date and version.

sections.title: 5. Payments, bonds, zero custody
sections.body:
The platform never holds funds. Every economic commitment — leg payment, carrier bond, hub bond, early-pickup payment — is a conditional payment (hold invoice) directly between the two users involved; hub fees are instant payments at the handoff. The platform only holds the information that releases payments already locked towards beneficiaries fixed in advance, never money: if the instance stops working, every hold expires and the funds return to the payers automatically.

The sender's offer is a spending commitment paid leg by leg: 90% is the work pool, split in proportion to the kilometres of approach; 10% is the finalization bonus (70% to the carrier who delivers to the destination, 30% to the destination hub at pickup). Every leg's amounts are computed and frozen at acceptance: no renegotiation.

Whoever takes custody of the parcel locks the bond set by the sender — at most €1,000 — which is released when custody passes with certification, and is collected by the sender only in the outcomes of §8. All amounts are denominated and settled in satoshi; the euro is indicative only, at the rate frozen when the shipment was created: whoever earns sats accepts the sats exchange risk. Every movement is recorded in a double-entry ledger.

sections.title: 6. Contents: self-declaration, exclusions, value cap
sections.body:
The sender self-declares that the contents are lawful and remains solely responsible for them. Hubs and carriers are mere holders in good faith: they take custody of a sealed parcel on the strength of the sender's declarations and can always refuse it, without penalty (§7).

It is forbidden to ship: weapons, ammunition and explosives; narcotic or psychotropic substances; medicines; tobacco and excise goods; cash, bearer instruments, prepaid cards and valuables; live animals; dangerous, flammable or corrosive materials, including lithium batteries not installed or not protected; counterfeit or unlawfully sourced goods; any good whose shipment between private parties is forbidden by law. Perishable goods are allowed but travel at the sender's sole risk.

The value of the contents may not exceed €45 (a threshold aligned with the EU customs relief for occasional consignments between private individuals). The cap also serves as the objective valuation criterion in the end-of-storage release (§10). The value cap is not a care cap: the required bond can reach €1,000 (§5).

Declaring the contents is optional: parcels with undeclared contents only reach hubs that explicitly accept them, and are flagged on the board before a carrier accepts. The platform performs no general monitoring of the contents it coordinates; the safeguard is the documented right of refusal (§7) and the traceability of the custody chain.

sections.title: 7. Handoffs: accept or refuse, never judge
sections.body:
At every handoff, whoever receives the parcel has exactly two moves: accept — certifying its integrity with photos; custody passes and the previous custodian's bond and payouts are released for good — or refuse, documenting it with photos and a reason: custody does NOT pass, the state does not change, no money moves and the sender is notified.

Liability follows certified custody: whoever accepts without inspecting assumes the risk. From the moment of certification, any damage discovered later is attributed to the current custodian, whose bond is the only guarantee at stake. Refusal is free and carries no penalty; it is recorded in the custody chain and, if systematic and unjustified, weighs on reputation (§11).

The sender can document the initial state with optional photos of the contents and of the sealed parcel at creation: they are the sender's main documentary protection.

sections.title: 8. Deterministic outcomes: no arbiter, no dispute
9. Pickup, final acceptance, early pickup
sections.body:
At destination the recipient inspects the parcel and then enters the OTP code received by email: that is the final acceptance and closes the shipment. There is no contestation window after pickup; before entering the code the recipient can refuse the handoff like anyone else (§7). Pickup is free.

Early pickup: with the personal code received in the tracking email, a recipient holding an account with a wallet can claim the parcel while it rests at a hub along the route, collecting the residual compensation: they are doing the last leg themselves. The physical pickup is final acceptance, like the OTP. The personal code is a bearer credential: whoever receives it must not share it.

Early pickup does not pause storage: if storage expires before the pickup, the parcel is released (§10) and the early-pickup payments are cancelled.

sections.title: 10. End of storage: release to the hub
sections.body:
The sender chooses at creation the maximum storage time per hub (up to 7 days); the system rejects hubs with a shorter limit and the current deadline is always visible on the shipment and tracking pages. When it expires, the good itself compensates the hub for the storage: with the no-custody architecture there is no fund to forward. The release operates as a cascade:

1. Warnings: sender and recipient receive a warning email 72 and 24 hours before the deadline. Until the deadline the sender can always get the parcel moving again (boost), change its destination or recall it towards the origin (reroute), or cancel the shipment if no carrier has picked it up yet.
2. Expiry: the shipment enters the Released state, the hub's bond is freed and the platform's coordination ends; the following phases are direct obligations between hub and sender, governed by these Terms and performed outside the software.
3. Recovery window — 7 days: for 7 calendar days from the expiry the sender (or someone they appoint) can still collect the parcel at the hub, paying the accrued extra storage directly to the hub. The hub is bound to hand it over against payment.
4. Extra storage rate: €1.50 for every started day past the deadline (1/30 of the value cap), payable in sats at the current rate or in cash, directly to the hub. The rate is uniform and fixed here ex ante.
5. Release in 'marcian' form: once the recovery window has lapsed, the hub may keep or realize the good to offset the accrued extra-storage credit. The valuation is anchored to the declarable value cap (€45, §6); if the value so estimated exceeds the accrued credit, the excess is owed to the sender. Alternatively the hub may opt for a documented donation to a charity, which extinguishes the mutual claims; disposal is allowed only for perishable or worthless goods, with a recorded justification.

The clause mirrors powers the law already grants to those who store or transport (artt. 1686, 1690, 2756, 2796-2797 of the Italian Civil Code), with extra safeguards: warnings, a recovery window, recall always available before expiry, a rate published ex ante, a valuation cap and any excess owed to the sender. This clause is specifically approved under artt. 1341-1342 of the Italian Civil Code (§15).

sections.title: 11. Reviews
sections.body: Once a shipment is closed — whatever the outcome, including bad ones — every effective participant can review every other effective participant, in the role they actually had, within 30 days of closure. Ratings are separate per role (sender, carrier, hub); reviews are public, cannot be edited and cannot be deleted: they are a dated judgment, like a custody event. In a system without arbiters, reputation is the sanction: whoever publishes a review is responsible for what they write; defamatory content and third parties' personal data are forbidden.

sections.title: 12. The Operator's obligations and liability
sections.body:
The software is provided "as is", with no guarantee of continuous availability (MIT license). The Operator is not liable for the users' obligations towards each other (transport, custody, delivery, payments) nor for damage from unforeseeable events, force majeure or misuse of the service.

The Operator has no discretionary power over money movements: every movement is decided by the rules of these Terms and executed by the software. The architecture guarantees that even if the instance is compromised or shut down, locked funds return to the payers or go to the beneficiaries already fixed (§5).

Nothing in these Terms excludes or limits the Operator's liability where the law does not allow it. The Operator may suspend accounts that violate §6 or use the service for unlawful activities; suspension never touches locked funds, which follow the outcomes of §8 regardless.

sections.title: 13. Personal data
sections.body: The processing of personal data is described in the privacy policy, which also covers the position of the recipient designated by the sender. The custody chain and the ledger contain no personal data and survive account deletion (anonymization).

sections.title: 14. Amendments, governing law, venue
sections.body:
The Operator may update these Terms; changes apply to new sign-ups and, for existing accounts, after email notice with 30 days' warning. Shipments in progress remain governed by the version accepted when they were created.

These Terms are governed by Italian law. For consumer users the competent venue is the consumer's place of residence or domicile; for other users, the venue of the Operator's seat.

sections.title: 15. Specifically approved clauses (artt. 1341-1342 Italian Civil Code)
sections.body:
Under artt. 1341 and 1342 of the Italian Civil Code, the user specifically approves the following clauses: §3 (the Operator not being a party to the contracts between users); §7 (liability follows certified custody); §8 (deterministic outcomes, no arbiter, no compensation in unprovable cases, no insurance); §9 (pickup as final acceptance, no contestation window); §10 (release of the parcel at the end of storage, extra storage rate, valuation and realization in marcian form); §12 (the Operator's liability limitations); §14 (amendments and venue).

The approval happens with the dedicated checkbox at account creation, distinct from the general acceptance, and is recorded with date and version.

sections.title: 1. Data controller
sections.body: The controller is the Operator of this Mercurio instance, identified — with contact details — at the bottom of this page. Mercurio is open source software: each instance has its own controller.

sections.title: 2. Who this policy addresses
sections.body:
– Registered users (senders, carriers, hubs, recipients collecting early): the policy is provided under art. 13 GDPR at account creation, where its acceptance is recorded.
– Unregistered recipients: the sender enters the recipient's email for the shipment's notifications. For them the policy is provided under art. 14 GDPR at first contact: the shipment's first email, and every following one, link to this page. Source of the data: the sender. Categories of data: the email address, nothing else.

sections.title: 3. What data, for what purposes, on what bases
sections.body:
– Email, language, sign-up date and recorded consents: account, magic-link authentication, operational notifications — performance of the contract (art. 6(1)(b)); kept until account deletion.
– Hub data (business name, address, coordinates, opening hours, constraints, percentage): publication of the hub in the network, matching and leg computation — art. 6(1)(b); until deactivation or deletion.
– Carrier trips (origin, destination, deviation, rate): matching with the board — art. 6(1)(b); until deletion.
– Recipient's email (entered by the sender): shipment lifecycle notifications — legitimate interest in performing the shipment (art. 6(1)(f)); for the contract's parties, art. 6(1)(b).
– Shipments, legs, storage stays and custody chain: coordination and documentary proof — art. 6(1)(b); the chain contains no personal data and remains after deletion.
– Double-entry ledger: traceability and reconciliation of observed movements — art. 6(1)(b) and legitimate interest in the system's integrity (art. 6(1)(f)); kept without personal data after deletion.
– Photos (contents, sealed parcel, handoffs): the parties' documentary protection — art. 6(1)(b); for the counterparties of an ongoing custody, art. 6(1)(f); kept at most 90 days from upload, or up to 30 days after the shipment closes if earlier (§5).
– Wallet connection string: execution of the payments the user requests from their own wallet — art. 6(1)(b); encrypted at rest, removed at deletion.
– Reviews: public per-role reputation — art. 6(1)(b); permanent, attached to an anonymized identifier after deletion.

There is no processing for marketing, profiling or automated decisions about people: the only automatisms (the state machine's deterministic outcomes) concern the shipment and are described in the Terms of Service (§8).

sections.title: 4. Minimization by design
5. Photos: the full lifecycle
sections.body:
1. The photo is re-encoded and stripped of metadata on the device, then its sha256 fingerprint is computed and enters the custody chain.
2. The bytes are uploaded only after certification and served only via the API to the shipment's participants, with the session: never public URLs.
3. Every photo is born with a deletion date: at most 90 days from upload; when the shipment closes, the date is tightened to closure + 30 days. A daily process deletes expired photos (art. 5(1)(e) GDPR).
4. On account deletion, photos the user took for already-closed shipments are deleted immediately; those of shipments still in progress remain until their natural expiry, as the counterparties' documentary protection (art. 6(1)(f)).

sections.title: 6. Data recipients and transfers
sections.body:
Data lives in the Operator's infrastructure; transactional emails are sent through the SMTP provider indicated at the bottom of this page. There are no other recipients: no selling, no third-party analytics.

The carrier's route map loads OpenStreetMap tiles: the browser contacts the OSM servers (which see the IP address) only when the map is open. The export to Google Maps happens only if the user clicks the link. Any transfers outside the EU depend on the providers chosen by the instance and are indicated at the bottom of this page.

sections.title: 7. Your rights
sections.body:
– Access and portability (artt. 15 and 20): from the Account page you download in one click the complete JSON export of everything the instance keeps about you — account, hub, trips, shipments, reviews, consent history.
– Erasure (art. 17): from the same page, with explicit confirmation. Erasure anonymizes: email and personal data are unlinked forever and sessions revoked; the ledger and the custody chain remain — they contain no personal data — attached to an anonymized identifier (§8).
– Objection (art. 21): a recipient who does not want a shipment's emails can object at any time by writing to the controller; every shipment email links to this policy. The objection stops the notifications, not the physical shipment already entrusted to the network.
– Rectification (art. 16) and every other right: by writing to the controller.
– Complaint: to the supervisory authority (for Italy, the Garante per la protezione dei dati personali — gpdp.it).

sections.title: 8. Deletion: what remains and why
sections.body: Account deletion removes or unlinks all personal data; what remains, anonymized, are the facts the system must be able to prove towards the other users too: the ledger entries (observed movements), the custody chain events (who certified what, as an anonymized identifier) and the published reviews. Any hub is deactivated. Photos follow §5.

sections.title: 9. Security
sections.body: The keys that release payments and the wallets' connection secrets are encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM); the platform never has access to the funds: even a full database compromise cannot divert money to third parties. Pickup codes and personal codes are stored as hashes only, and the verification routes are rate limited. The session is an httpOnly cookie; photos are served only with session authorization on every request.

sections.title: 10. Minors
sections.body: The service is restricted to adults (Terms of Service §4); no processing of minors' data is envisaged.

sections.title: 11. Changes
sections.body: Changes to this policy are published on this page with a new version date; for substantial changes registered users are notified by email.

track.title: Your parcel
track.intro: Follow the shipment from this page and, if it suits you, pick it up early at any hub along the route: you do the last mile and you collect the remaining compensation.
track.loginNote: Sign in (or create an account) with your email to continue. The personal code from the tracking email remains your credential: do not share it.
track.notYetBody: Paste the personal code from the "your parcel is on its way" email. The amounts are computed and frozen by the system at request time: no surprises. A connected Lightning wallet is required: you are the one collecting.
track.needWallet: Collecting requires a Lightning wallet connected to your account.
track.tokenHint: It is in the tracking email. Whoever holds it can claim the parcel: do not share it.
track.fundingBy: The sender's wallet must lock the funds by {time}; then the claim becomes active and you can go pick up.
track.claimPending: Claim awaiting funding: the window is 60 minutes from the request. Refresh shortly.
track.claimExpired: The last claim expired unfunded: you can try again.
track.claimActive: Claim active: go to the hub and present your personal code to pick up. Inspect the parcel before accepting: acceptance is definitive.
track.tokenQrHint: The hub scans or types it at the counter.

carrierOps.title: Carrier actions
carrierOps.checkoutIntro: At the hub's counter? Confirm the pickup: it counts together with the hub's confirmation, within 15 minutes of each other. When custody passes you pay the handing hub's fee on the spot.
carrierOps.qrHint: Scan the QR on the parcel and paste the result here (the full URL works too).
carrierOps.checkoutWaitingHub: Confirmation recorded: waiting for the hub's confirmation.
carrierOps.checkoutComplete: Pickup complete: the parcel is in your custody, deliver within the transit deadline.
carrierOps.rejectIntro: If the parcel does not match what the board showed, document it with photos and a reason: custody stays with the hub and the sender is notified.
carrierOps.rejectDone: Rejection filed: the sender has been notified.

photos.label: Parcel photos
photos.hint: Photos are stripped of metadata (location, device) on your device before sending: the sha256 fingerprint certifies exactly the uploaded bytes. Maximum {max}.
photos.ready: {count, plural, one {1 photo certified} other {# photos certified}}
photos.decodeError: One of the photos could not be processed: try a different file.
photos.uploadFailed: {failed, plural, one {1 photo was not uploaded} other {# photos were not uploaded}} — the certification still stands.
photos.galleryIntro: Photos uploaded at handoffs: compare the parcel's state before accepting it.

qrScan.aim: Center the QR code in the box.
qrScan.privacyNote: The images stay on your device: nothing is sent or stored.
qrScan.errorInsecure: The camera needs a secure connection (https). Paste the QR result here instead.
qrScan.errorDenied: Camera permission denied. Enable it in your browser settings, or paste the QR result here.
qrScan.errorNotFound: No camera available. Paste the QR result here instead.
qrScan.errorGeneric: Could not start the camera. Paste the QR result here instead.

apiErrors.rate_limited: Too many attempts: try again later.
apiErrors.conflict: Another operation is in progress: reload and retry.
apiErrors.illegal_event: Action not allowed in the shipment's current state.
apiErrors.wallet_unavailable: The wallet is unreachable: check your wallet connection.
apiErrors.wallet_required: A connected wallet is required for this operation.
apiErrors.self_payment_impossible: On Lightning payer and payee must be different users: you cannot be your own counterparty.
apiErrors.hubs_too_close: The two hubs are too close.
apiErrors.sender_owns_hub: You cannot use your own hub as origin or destination.
apiErrors.bond_above_cap: The bond exceeds the allowed maximum (€1,000).
apiErrors.parcel_too_big: The parcel exceeds the hub's maximum dimensions.
apiErrors.parcel_too_heavy: The parcel exceeds the hub's maximum weight.
apiErrors.undeclared_not_accepted: This hub does not accept parcels with undeclared content.
apiErrors.hub_storage_too_short: The requested storage exceeds the hub's maximum.
apiErrors.trip_not_active: The trip is no longer active: declare a new one.
apiErrors.trip_expires_before_departure: The trip expiry precedes the departure.
apiErrors.not_at_hub: The parcel is not idle at a hub.
apiErrors.not_the_sender: Only the sender can do this.
apiErrors.fake_wallets_disabled: Fake wallets are not enabled on this API.
apiErrors.nwc_invalid_connection_string: Invalid NWC connection string: it must look like nostr+walletconnect://…
apiErrors.nwc_connection_failed: Could not reach the wallet: check the connection string and try again.
apiErrors.nwc_missing_required_methods: This wallet doesn't support the minimum required methods (pay_invoice, make_invoice, lookup_invoice).
apiErrors.wallet_missing_hold_support: This wallet doesn't support hold invoices: connect a compatible wallet (e.g. Alby Hub) to use this feature.
apiErrors.preview_pair_required: Incomplete preview: both shipment and drop hub are required.
apiErrors.pool_exhausted: The remaining work pool is exhausted: a boost is needed.
apiErrors.progress_below_minimum: The leg's progress is below the minimum threshold.
apiErrors.not_a_hub: This account does not run a hub.
apiErrors.hub_already_exists: You already registered a hub with this account.
apiErrors.not_origin_hub: Only the shipment's origin hub can do this.
apiErrors.not_custodian_hub: Only the hub holding the parcel can do this.
apiErrors.qr_mismatch: The scanned QR does not match this shipment.
apiErrors.no_booked_leg: No booked leg to pick up.
apiErrors.not_a_checkout_party: Only the handing hub or the leg's carrier can confirm the check-out.
apiErrors.checkout_photo_required: The hub's confirmation requires at least one photo.
apiErrors.no_leg_in_transit: No leg is in transit for this shipment.
apiErrors.not_arrival_hub: Only the leg's arrival hub can check the parcel in.
apiErrors.not_departure_hub: Only the leg's departure hub can take the parcel back.
apiErrors.not_the_receiving_party: Only the party receiving the parcel can reject the handoff.
apiErrors.parcel_not_idle_at_hub: The parcel is not idle at a hub.
apiErrors.guard_failed: The action violates the protocol rules in the current state.
apiErrors.hold_not_held: The wallet did not lock the required amount: try again.
apiErrors.instant_payment_failed: The instant fee payment failed: try again.
apiErrors.shipment_not_closed: You can review only after the shipment is closed.
apiErrors.review_window_closed: The review window (30 days from closure) has expired.
apiErrors.not_a_participant: Only participants of the shipment can write reviews.
apiErrors.subject_role_not_effective: This user did not hold that role in the shipment.
apiErrors.already_reviewed: You already reviewed this user in this role.
apiErrors.photo_not_certified: This photo does not match any certified fingerprint of the shipment.
apiErrors.photo_not_photographer: Only the user who certified the photo can upload it.
apiErrors.photo_hash_mismatch: The uploaded bytes do not match the declared fingerprint.
apiErrors.photo_format_unsupported: Unsupported format: only JPEG is accepted.
apiErrors.photo_exif_gps: The photo carries GPS coordinates in its metadata: strip them and retry.
